title: ETS iteration and folding
purpose: ETS tables need to be iterable — `tab2list/1` dumps all entries, `foldl/3` accumulates over entries, and `first/next/last/prev` provide cursor-based traversal for ordered_set tables. OTP uses these for diagnostics, serialization, and ordered iteration.
task: Implement iteration and folding BIFs for all table types.

requirements.title: ets:tab2list/1
acceptance: Returns a list of all tuples in the table
Set/bag: unordered. Ordered_set: sorted by key.
Test: insert 3 entries, tab2list returns all 3

requirements.title: ets:foldl/3
acceptance: `ets:foldl(Fun, Acc0, Tab)` folds over all entries with a function
Fun receives (Element, AccIn) and returns AccOut
Uses the trampoline calling convention (B-038 R3) for closure invocation
Test: foldl to sum values in a table

requirements.title: ets:first/1, ets:next/2, ets:last/1, ets:prev/2
acceptance: `first(Tab)` returns smallest key (ordered_set) or any key (set/bag)
`next(Tab, Key)` returns next key, or `'$end_of_table'` atom at end
For non-ordered tables: first/next provide arbitrary-but-complete traversal
Test: ordered_set traversal via first/next visits all keys in order

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement safe iteration under concurrent modification — iteration sees a snapshot (or documents the caveat)
SHALL NOT implement ets:match_object or ets:select — those need match specs (B-097)
SHALL NOT implement continuation-based iteration in this brief
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
